title: Post Lifecycle Regression Fix Tasklist
mainGoal: Correct the regressions and incomplete behaviors surfaced by the March 24, 2026 full lifecycle execution so Ehecatl can complete install, runtime boot, smoke, shutdown, uninstall, purge, and rerun flows without timeout-driven failures or misleading operator outcomes.

tasklistPolicy.orderingRule: Fix the runtime blockers first, then shutdown and uninstall correctness, then observability gaps, and finally rerun-safety and regression coverage.
tasklistPolicy.focusRule: Prioritize real lifecycle failures observed in execution over speculative cleanup. Every fix should end in a rerunnable regression check or a new automated test when practical.
tasklistPolicy.readerNote: This tasklist is derived directly from the latest executed lifecycle report. It is not a general backlog. Each task maps to a concrete failure or gap observed during the March 24, 2026 run.

sourceReports.topic: Executed lifecycle campaign with fresh install, runtime, stop, uninstall, purge, and rerun evidence
Tasklist that drove the executed lifecycle campaign
Published setup, uninstall, and purge contract

taskGroups.title: Restore Stable Runtime Boot Under `concurrentInstances = "max"`
taskGroups.goal: Make the runtime boot stably when engine concurrency resolves to the available CPU core count, without causing manager heartbeat timeouts or route-resolution stalls.
pointsOfAttention: The latest lifecycle run showed two engines on this host and repeated manager `heartbeat_timeout` while tenancy routes were still loading.
Fixes here must prefer real runtime behavior over theoretical support for `"max"`.

tasks.title: Find why manager bootstrap misses heartbeat deadlines under the new engine concurrency setting

subtasks.title: Trace manager bootstrap timing from process spawn through tenancy route loading and first heartbeat emission
Confirm whether the failure is caused by slow tenancy scan, blocked privilege transition, shared resource contention, or supervisor timing assumptions
Decide whether the correct fix is in bootstrap ordering, heartbeat timeout policy, tenancy scan timing, or `"max"` engine launch fan-out

tasks.title: Implement the runtime fix for stable manager readiness

subtasks.title: Change the relevant bootstrap, heartbeat, or startup sequencing so manager readiness no longer races into `heartbeat_timeout` during normal route loading
Preserve support for `concurrentInstances = "max"` or intentionally constrain it with explicit logic if full fan-out is not safe yet
Keep log messages specific enough to distinguish slow manager startup from crashed manager startup

tasks.title: Add a regression test for the runtime stability issue

subtasks.title: Add an automated test that exercises startup timing or readiness behavior relevant to the manager timeout regression
Cover the intended behavior when engine concurrency resolves above `1`
Make the test fail on the old heartbeat-timeout behavior and pass with the fix

taskGroups.title: Restore Successful Smoke Path And Route Resolution
taskGroups.goal: Make loopback smoke traffic complete successfully again for redirect, static, cached, controller, and session routes under the current baseline.
pointsOfAttention: The latest lifecycle run timed out at the client and later logged `RPC ask timeout after 30000ms` for route resolution.
This is a correctness blocker, not just a performance issue.

subtasks.title: Trace one failing request from engine acceptance through manager route lookup and identify where the request waits for 30000ms
Fix the blocking condition so route resolution returns promptly during healthy startup
Ensure timeout-driven route failures do not silently look like successful request completion in logs

tasks.title: Revalidate the full smoke matrix after the runtime fix

subtasks.title: Re-run `/`, `/htm/index.htm`, `/htm/cached.htm`, `/hello`, `/session`, and `POST /post-data` for both `fakedomain.com` and `www.fakedomain.com`
Re-run one negative-path matrix for missing asset, missing controller, invalid method, unsupported content type, and invalid CSRF
Update the latest performance report or create a fresh rerun report with the corrected outcomes

taskGroups.title: Fix Shutdown And Stop Timeout Behavior
taskGroups.goal: Make `npm run stop` and service stop complete cleanly without systemd timeout kills or manager restart churn during deactivation.

tasks.title: Stop manager restarts during coordinated shutdown

subtasks.title: Trace why the manager is relaunched while the service is already deactivating
Prevent restart-on-exit logic from fighting an intentional shutdown path
Keep shutdown logs explicit about intentional stop versus crash recovery

tasks.title: Align runtime teardown with systemd timeout expectations

subtasks.title: Ensure child teardown finishes before `TimeoutStopSec` under normal conditions or adjust the service contract if the runtime legitimately needs longer
Verify stop leaves the service in `inactive` rather than `failed`
Add regression coverage for the previously observed stop timeout and forced kill path

taskGroups.title: Fix Uninstall And Rerun Safety
taskGroups.goal: Make uninstall remove the project tree as documented and prevent leftover `/opt/ehecatl` state from forcing reruns to depend on `--force`.

tasks.title: Fix uninstall so project removal matches the documented contract

subtasks.title: Trace why `/opt/ehecatl` remained after uninstall in the latest lifecycle run
Correct uninstall behavior so project files are actually removed while data-preservation boundaries remain intact
Add a verification step or automated test so leftover project trees fail uninstall validation

tasks.title: Fix rerun behavior after purge and uninstall

subtasks.title: Re-run installer after uninstall and purge without `--force` and confirm it follows the intended fresh-install path
Only keep `--force` as an intentional operator override, not as a hidden dependency on cleanup residue
Update docs if the intended contract changes

taskGroups.title: Close Observability Gaps Exposed By The Lifecycle Run
taskGroups.goal: Make the lifecycle artifacts consistent so logs, reports, and request outcomes all tell a truthful and complete story.

subtasks.title: Trace why `logger-runtime` did not emit `src/report.json` during either runtime pass
Decide whether the report should be written on failed requests, successful requests, or both, and implement that contract consistently
Add regression coverage proving `src/report.json` appears under the tenant `src/` folder when request traffic is present

tasks.title: Make request outcome logging reflect real failures

subtasks.title: Review why route-resolution timeout cases still logged request completion with status `200`
Make runtime logs expose the real client-visible result and the internal failure cause together
Verify error and runtime logs remain easy to correlate by request or timing context

taskGroups.title: Harden `setup --force` Around Existing Scaffold Hosts
taskGroups.goal: Prevent a routine `setup --force` from failing just because the first-tenant host already exists.

tasks.title: Define the intended force-setup behavior when scaffold tenant already exists

subtasks.title: Choose whether existing scaffold host should be skipped, refreshed idempotently, or treated as an optional warning
Keep `--force` focused on reapplying setup and service provisioning rather than blocking on tenant recreation
Document the chosen behavior in setup docs if it differs from current prompts

tasks.title: Implement and validate the idempotent scaffold behavior

subtasks.title: Update the setup script to handle an existing first-tenant scaffold safely under `--force`
Add a regression test or scripted verification for the exact failure observed in the lifecycle run
Verify `setup --force` now completes successfully whether the tenant exists or not

taskGroups.title: Re-run The Full Lifecycle Campaign After Fixes
taskGroups.goal: Close the loop by proving the observed failures are actually corrected in the real operator-facing lifecycle.

tasks.title: Execute a new disposable lifecycle rerun after the fixes

subtasks.title: Use the same broad lifecycle tasklist shape so results stay comparable
Confirm runtime boot, smoke, stop, uninstall, purge, and rerun all pass without timeout-driven workarounds
Capture the corrected timings and note whether `"max"` concurrency remains the chosen default
Create one new `report/performance` JSON report dedicated to the fixed rerun
Compare the new results explicitly against the March 24 failure report
State clearly which previously failing categories are now resolved and which, if any, remain open

finalArtifacts: Code fixes for the runtime, shutdown, uninstall, setup-force, and observability regressions
Regression tests or scripted verifications for the newly fixed lifecycle failures
One fresh lifecycle rerun report proving the corrections in the real operator-facing flow
